purpose: Lock the One Thing into a short, repeatable statement. Synthesize the arc. No new fact; no new build. The canonical 4 × 5 = 20 rectangle finishes where it started.
trigger: S.1 runs after EC.1 returns Strong (or after the Needs Practice re-pass reaches Strong). If Still Gathering, S.1 is delivered after the Heavy remediation re-pass succeeds.
toy: §2.10 Drop Down + Mode C paired display (canonical 4 × 5 = 20 paired with 20 = 4 × 5 visible above the stem).
tutor_prompt_student_facing: "Say it one more time. These two equations are ___." (Mode C strip displays the canonical 4 × 5 = 20 paired with 20 = 4 × 5 one final time.)
required_closure_stem: I built a 4-by-5 rectangle, so I can write 4 × 5 = 20 AND 20 = 4 × 5 — they say the same thing, and the equal sign means both sides have the same value.
divider: Drop Down options (student selects to complete "These two equations are ___"): "the same fact written two ways" / "a wrong one and a right one" / "two different facts." Only "the same fact written two ways" is correct.
teacher_move: Post-completion (observable): "You can write it both ways. The equal sign means both sides have the same value — and that's true no matter which way you write it."
